C-CONTEXT-TUI-DETAILED-ATTRIBUTION.IntentSummary: Extend the existing pure /context analysis into reconciled per-tool and per-MCP attribution, derive context-window percentages only from the current model limit, render the results through a responsive host-owned three-tab dialog, preserve active post-compaction and measured-versus-estimated semantics, document the public behavior, and synchronize the owning GRACE graph and verification projections.

Baseline.Note: The analyzer currently estimates aggregate built-in, vvoc, and external tool-schema categories and one combined Tool calls and results category, but it does not retain per-tool schema, call count, or history usage.
The analyzer already limits message and part accounting to the latest compaction summary and subsequent active turns, and skill tool history is separately owned by Loaded skill results.
The public OpenCode 1.18.2 tool catalog exposes tool id, description, and parameters, while active tool parts expose tool id, input, output or error, and state.
OpenCode 1.18.2 names MCP tools as sanitize(serverName) + "_" + sanitize(toolName); current MCP state exposes server names and statuses but ToolListItem has no direct source metadata.
The current host-owned xlarge dialog renders one static report and applies setSize after replace; view regression coverage protects this ordering and the absence of a nested Dialog wrapper.
README and GRACE projections describe aggregate estimates, status-only MCP visibility, and the current uncertainty boundary that forbids guessed ownership.

Target.Note: One pure detailed analysis model owns category percentages, per-tool schema and active-history metrics, deterministic source classification, reconciled subtotals, and per-server MCP aggregates.
Unique longest-prefix MCP matching is treated as the supported current OpenCode naming contract; sanitized collisions remain unattributed and produce bounded warnings.
The dialog keeps provider-measured usage visible while exposing Overview, Tools, and MCP tabs with responsive scrolling and stable keyboard navigation.
Direct Solid reactive state used by the TUI view is declared through package metadata compatible with the existing OpenTUI 0.4.3 peer surface.
Public documentation and GRACE current-state artifacts explain active scope, percentages, disconnected-server schema behavior, retained active history, and non-exact estimates.

T-001.Title: Define detailed context attribution types and deterministic calculation helpers.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Update types.ts contracts and exports with reusable token metric, tool source, per-tool usage, and per-MCP aggregate shapes without exposing provider-exact claims.
Every estimated metric carries tokens and an optional percentage derived only from a positive model contextLimit; missing or invalid limits produce undefined percentage data for em-dash rendering.
Tool source classification distinguishes known built-in IDs, known vvoc IDs, uniquely matched MCP servers, and Other external/plugin.
MCP name sanitization matches OpenCode's non-alphanumeric replacement rule, candidates use longest-prefix selection, and equal longest sanitized matches are explicitly ambiguous.
Stable sorting orders tool and server detail by combined total descending with a deterministic name tie-breaker.
MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, and CHANGE_SUMMARY remain accurate for changed type and analyzer responsibilities.

T-002.Title: Aggregate active per-tool schema and history usage with reconciled MCP server totals.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Initialize tool detail from the current tool catalog so unused schemas remain visible with zero calls and zero history.

Merge active post-compaction tool parts by exact tool ID, count each observable call once, and estimate history from tool ID, input, completed output, or error using the existing provider-neutral estimator.
Pending and running calls contribute their currently observable input, while completed attachments continue to be owned by Files and attachments as before.
The skill tool is present in tool detail, but its history remains in Loaded skill results and is not added a second time to Tool calls and results.
Detailed schema subtotals exactly reconcile with the three existing schema categories, and detailed history reconciles with Tool calls and results plus the separately reported skill history.
Connected servers aggregate uniquely attributed current schemas and active history; non-connected statuses receive zero current schema but retain matching active history.
Ambiguous MCP ownership leaves the tool in Other external/plugin and appends one bounded warning rather than assigning it speculatively.
No new client or MCP request is added; analyzeContext continues to consume the existing tool catalog, active messages and parts, model metadata, and MCP status snapshot.

T-003.Title: Strengthen pure analyzer regression coverage for percentages, tools, MCP attribution, and compaction.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Tests cover percentages against a positive context limit and undefined percentages when the limit is absent or non-positive.
Tests cover an unused schema, repeated calls, completed outputs, errors, pending inputs, stable sorting, and schema plus history total calculation.
Tests prove pre-compaction calls are excluded and post-compaction calls remain counted.
Tests prove skill detail does not double-count overview history and all schema and history reconciliation invariants hold.
Tests cover connected, disabled, failed, needs_auth, and needs_client_registration MCP status behavior, including retained active history with zero disconnected schema.
Tests cover longest-prefix server selection, sanitized-name collisions, bounded ambiguity warnings, and Other external/plugin fallback.
Update analyze.test.ts MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, and CHANGE_SUMMARY for the expanded deterministic scenarios.

T-004.Title: Implement the responsive Overview, Tools, and MCP dialog views with keyboard navigation and scrolling.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Preserve the direct host dialog composition and replace-before-setSize ordering while keeping the measured usage header visible across all tabs.
Add component-local tab state with Overview as the initial tab; left and right cycle tabs and keys 1, 2, and 3 select them directly only while the context dialog is active.
Use a focused vertical scrollbox with terminal-relative bounded height so long tool and MCP lists remain navigable without extending beyond normal or narrow terminal viewports.
Overview shows each category's estimate, percentage or em dash, and a bounded bar whose fill clamps at 100 percent without clamping the numeric percentage.
Tools shows source, active calls, schema, history, total, and percentages for every detailed tool in analyzer order.
MCP shows canonical statuses, tool counts, aggregate metrics, nested tool detail, and an explicit Other external/plugin section.
Long names and unavailable values wrap or align predictably without clipping the dialog horizontally.
Declare SolidJS 1.9.12 as a compatible direct development and peer requirement for reactive view state, update bun.lock, and preserve existing OpenTUI peer versions.
Dialog cleanup removes any dialog-local keymap registration, while host Escape dismissal and subsequent /context openings do not leak bindings or stale tab state.
Update view.tsx MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, block anchors, and CHANGE_SUMMARY to describe the tabbed host-owned view.

T-005.Title: Expand view regression coverage for tab transitions, rendering, scrolling, and dialog lifecycle.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Preserve regression checks for no nested Dialog wrapper and replace-before-xlarge sizing.
Tests exercise deterministic tab transition helpers and dialog-local binding cleanup without requiring a running OpenCode process.
OpenTUI test rendering or an equivalently deterministic rendering boundary captures Overview, Tools, and MCP labels and representative metrics.
A bounded-height test with enough rows to overflow proves the scrollable body remains within the rendered frame and responds to vertical navigation.
Tests cover percentages, em dashes, above-100 numeric values with clamped bars, disconnected MCP schema zero, retained history, and Other external/plugin output.
Update view.test.ts MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, and CHANGE_SUMMARY for the expanded presentation verification surface.

T-006.Title: Document detailed /context semantics and synchronize source contracts.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: README describes all three tabs, context-limit percentage semantics, active post-compaction scope, schema versus history, disabled-server schema zero, retained active history, and Other external/plugin uncertainty.
README continues to distinguish provider-measured usage from provider-neutral estimates and does not promise exact provider payload reconstruction.
Technology context records the direct SolidJS reactive-state peer/development contract if introduced by the implementation.
All edited source and test MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, CHANGE_SUMMARY, and block anchors are accurate and paired.
No setup command, config schema, plugin toggle, package export, or generated dist behavior changes.

T-007.Title: Synchronize GRACE requirements, context-inspection flow, module ownership, and verification.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: UC-021 and the current attribution non-goal describe tabbed detail, context-limit percentages, active scope, deterministic MCP naming, and fail-closed ambiguity without weakening estimate honesty.
M-PLUGIN-CONTEXT-TUI describes detailed tool and MCP analysis, tabbed rendering, and any new Solid/keymap dependency while retaining the existing owned paths.
DF-CONTEXT-INSPECTION describes reuse of existing catalogs and active parts through detailed analysis into the bounded tabbed dialog.
VF-CONTEXT-INSPECTION and V-M-PLUGIN-CONTEXT-TUI require percentage, reconciliation, MCP attribution, tab, scrolling, and failure scenarios with the actual focused test files and gates.
No new graph or verification anchor is introduced unless fresh implementation evidence proves the existing module boundary is insufficient.

T-008.Title: Run final repository gates and live normal-width and narrow-width TUI verification.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Focused context tests and the full repository check pass with fresh output.
Build and pack checks pass with the declared Solid/OpenTUI package surface and all public exports remain importable.
GRACE lint reports zero errors and warnings, status recognizes the approved change and plan, and the final diff stays inside DurableScope and ObservedWriteScope.

An isolated OpenCode 1.18.2 smoke test with the locally built TUI opens /context, switches among all three tabs through both navigation forms, scrolls overflowing rows, closes through Escape, and reopens without stale state.
Normal and narrow terminal screenshots or equivalent captured frames show no horizontal clipping, bottom overflow, nested dialog, or unreadable value wrapping.
Any skipped or blocked command is reported explicitly; generated dist remains unedited manually.
